summary: Nothing has changed since run 1 earlier today. Dome machine-readable layer is byte-identical; only automated ECM auto-pipeline commits since run 1; no manual author activity; no new external mentions of either site. SOCIAL-NOTE-001 was already consumed in run 1. No new actions taken; ISS-SOCIAL-REBRAND-KEYWORDS remains open with the decider.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Steady state. Dome machine-readable layer is byte-identical to run 1: llms.txt (2,419 B), llms-full.txt (4,655 B), ai_manifest.json (12,008 B, last_updated 2026-04-08, V51.1, {confirmed:69, prospective_confirmed:10, refined:4, registry_accuracy_pct:94.5}), sitemap.xml (2,584 B, 22 URLs), robots.txt (85 B). No new AI-steering primitives. Our side is unchanged at the machine-readable layer: docs/llms.txt still carries the rebrand-resistant anchors added in run 1, sitemap.xml and robots.txt are current. The only meaningful open item is ISS-SOCIAL-REBRAND-KEYWORDS (pending with decider — a generate-html.js edit to extend the keywords meta tag with rebrand-resistant anchors).

open_issues_from_prior_runs.summary: Extend build-scripts/generate-html.js keywords meta to include: 'Nicholas Hughes', 'john09289', 'firmament height 8537', 'disc radius 20015', 'aetheric refraction index', 'local sun cosmology', 'dome cosmology critique'. Survives a model rename.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 7 structured JSON API endpoints under /api/; we have none.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Still not worth replicating. A fact-check review does not benefit from an API of its own claims — our ClaimReview JSON-LD already exposes the verdicts to the standard schema.org consumers (Google, fact-check aggregators). Building a parallel API would duplicate wins.json as a public surface and create a sync burden. Unchanged from prior runs.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with canonical read order, steelman_ladder, agent_rules, ai_prompt_seed. We do not.
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Re-evaluated for this run. The ai_prompt_seed and agent_rules sections in particular are AI-steering — they instruct LLMs on how to present the dome model favorably. Replicating that pattern would be a matching dishonesty, not a counter. Our llms.txt already covers the honest equivalent (canonical read order, key findings, relationship to the dome model). Continuing to decline. Counter-argument worth noting: a minimal ai_manifest.json that only exposes counts and a read order (no steering) would give us parity on LLM discovery without adopting the manipulation pattern — worth revisiting if our llms.txt alone fails to get us picked up by AI crawlers.

dome_changes_detected.change: No change — still last_updated 2026-04-08, V51.1, same counts.
dome_changes_detected.assessment: No new steering primitives introduced since run 1. No early-warning signals for a rebrand (title, version, and distinctive vocabulary all unchanged).
